mechanism_narrative: A sustained downward trend in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.81% indicates accommodative policy stance
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following falling-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, elevated labor market slack signals weak aggregate demand and depressed consumer spending capacity, causing investors to discount forward earnings growth and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE currently at 6.1%, above trigger threshold
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE > 6.0%, forward 60d returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC is below 0.03 across two consecutive rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in RSAFS and HOUST jointly indicate contracting consumer retail activity and housing investment, a combined demand-side deterioration that markets price into equities over the subsequent month more strongly than either series alone would predict, producing negative forward 20-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 895.0 thousand units, RSAFS at 375473 million dollars
failure_condition: Joint decline instances show forward 20d returns positive in over 55% of cases, or the interaction term's marginal predictive power (measured by Rank IC improvement over single-series models) is not statistically distinguishable from zero over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery expansion regime, sustained positive month-over-month growth in INDPRO signals reaccelerating industrial output and capacity utilization, which forecasts corporate earnings recovery and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 110.30, consistent with mid-cycle industrial expansion
failure_condition: In non-recovery regimes or when FEDFUNDS is rising sharply, the same INDPRO trend signal fails to produce significantly positive 60d returns (p > 0.05), or Rank IC drops below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window within the recovery regime.

mechanism_narrative: A positive trailing trend in PAYEMS month-over-month changes reflects continued labor market strength and consumer income growth, which supports near-term risk appetite and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS trailing 3-month trend and forward 5d returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive PAYEMS trend periods do not show significantly higher mean 5d returns than negative trend periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPIAUCSL inflation rises above 3%, markets anticipate future monetary tightening via FEDFUNDS hikes, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 218.88 with low FEDFUNDS at 1.81%, suggesting inflation not yet triggering hikes
failure_condition: During periods when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3%, forward 20d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances despite the negative signal, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 across two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in GDPC1 quarterly growth and RSAFS monthly growth signals a synchronized broadening of aggregate demand across investment and consumption channels, a combination that predicts stronger corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter more reliably than either indicator alone.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized > 2% AND RSAFS MoM > 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11727.4 billion, RSAFS at 375473 million
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances produce forward 60d returns not significantly higher than the single-indicator baseline (p > 0.05), or the interaction Rank IC improvement is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound monetary regime where FEDFUNDS remains near historic lows, a month-over-month decline in INDPRO signals that monetary easing is insufficient to sustain industrial activity, undermining investor confidence in earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.81% consistent with near-zero policy regime
failure_condition: During ZLB periods, INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5d SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window within the ZLB regime.
